meaning_english: Something or someone that one vehemently dislikes. Synonyms: Abomination, Curse, Bugbear, Detestation, Antipathy.
A person who renounces a religious or political belief. Synonyms: Deserter, Renegade, Traitor, Defector, Turncoat.
An indicator or predictor of something. Synonyms: Harbinger, Herald, Indicator, Guide, Pace-setter.
Resembling or relating to twilight. Synonyms: Twilight, Dusky, Dim, Shadowy, Somber.
Raise doubts or objections or show reluctance. Synonyms: Object, Protest, Hesitate, Dissent, Pause.
A feeling of listlessness and dissatisfaction arising from a lack of occupation or excitement. Synonyms: Boredom, Tedium, Lassitude, Weariness.
Express vehement protest. Synonyms: Rant, Declaim, Inveigh, Rail, Denounce.
A person who enjoys eating and often eats too much. Synonyms: Glutton, Epicure, Gastronome, Foodie.
Leadership or dominance, especially by one country or social group over others. Synonyms: Dominance, Supremacy, Authority, Control.
Likely to arouse or incur resentment or anger in others. Synonyms: Unpleasant, Awkward, Discriminatory, Unjust.
Art, objects, or design considered to be in poor taste because of excessive garishness. Synonyms: Gaudy, Tawdry, Garish, Trashy.
Trivial or very small. Synonyms: Tiny, Minute, Miniature, Diminutive, Petty.
Sweet or musical; pleasant to hear. Synonyms: Dulcet, Euphonious, Tuneful, Silvery.
Having an extremely offensive smell; or very unpleasant. Synonyms: Malodorous, Fetid, Foul, Noxious.
Harsh criticism or censure. Synonyms: Ignominy, Infamy, Disgrace, Shame, Vilification.
Showing a lack of courage or determination. Synonyms: Cowardly, Timid, Fearful, Spineless.
Complaining in a petulant or whining manner. Synonyms: Whiny, Petulant, Grumbling, Captious.
An establishment or resumption of harmonious relations. Synonyms: Reconciliation, Reunion, Understanding.
Incite people to rebel against the authority of a state or monarch. Synonyms: Rebellious, Insurgent, Mutinous, Subversive.
Eager or quick to argue or fight. Synonyms: Aggressive, Defiant, Belligerent, Pugnacious.
Excessively flattering or oily. Synonyms: Sycophantic, Fawning, Groveling, Smarmy.
Relating to deep inward feelings rather than to the intellect. Synonyms: Instinctive, Innate, Gut, Intuitive.
Use flattery or coaxing in order to persuade someone to do something. Synonyms: Coax, Cajole, Inveigle, Charm.
The defining spirit or mood of a particular period of history. Synonyms: Spirit of the age, Trend, Era mood.
Difficult to understand; obscure. Synonyms: Recondite, Esoteric, Arcane, Cryptic.
Relating to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life. Synonyms: Rustic, Rural, Pastoral, Arcadian.
Mercy; lenience. Synonyms: Mercy, Lenience, Indulgence, Quarter.
Lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm. Synonyms: Haphazard, Aimless, Rambling, Erratic.
Mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper. Synonyms: Composure, Poise, Self-control, Calm.
Producing or capable of producing an abundance of offspring or new growth. Synonyms: Fertile, Fruitful, Productive, Prolific.
Relevant to a subject under consideration. Synonyms: Relevant, Pertinent, Applicable, Apposite.
Excessive pride or self-confidence. Synonyms: Arrogance, Conceit, Haughtiness, Vanity.
In an initial stage; beginning to happen or develop. Synonyms: Nascent, Emerging, Dawning, Initial.
Fond of or characterized by joking; humorous or playful. Synonyms: Humorous, Funny, Witty, Facetious.
Used in reference to an announcement of the end of something. Synonyms: Toll, Dirge, Death-bell.
Completely clear and transparent. Synonyms: Lucid, Transparent, Clear, Crystalline.
Larger or more generous than is usual or necessary. Synonyms: Generous, Charitable, Bountiful, Philanthropic.
Wicked or criminal. Synonyms: Villainous, Heinous, Sinister, Evil.
Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action. Synonyms: Obstinate, Stubborn, Unyielding, Adamant.
Relieving pain or alleviating a problem without dealing with the underlying cause. Synonyms: Soothing, Alleviating, Mitigating.
Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical. Synonyms: Visionary, Romantic, Impractical.
Bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing. Synonyms: Bitterness, Spite, Resentment, Malice.
Optimistic or positive, especially in an apparently bad situation. Synonyms: Optimistic, Hopeful, Buoyant, Positive.
Very weak or slight. Synonyms: Fragile, Flimsy, Shaky, Insubstantial.
Present, appearing, or found everywhere. Synonyms: Omnipresent, Pervasive, Universal.
Accorded a great deal of respect, especially because of age or wisdom. Synonyms: Revered, Honored, Esteemed, Respected.
Attractive or appealing in appearance or character. Synonyms: Charming, Appealing, Engaging, Captivating.
Relating to institutionalized hospitality or relations between host and guest. Synonyms: Hospitable, Welcoming.
Used to describe something that restricts your freedom. Synonyms: Bondage, Oppression, Slavery, Burden.
A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their ideals. Synonyms: Fanatic, Radical, Extremist, Activist.
